name.fr: Rewena paraoa (pain maori à la pomme de terre)

description.en: A wild-fermented Māori loaf built on a 'potato bug': mashed potato, flour and sugar left to bubble for days until it's alive with natural yeast. Folded into a simple dough and baked in a tin, it comes out soft, faintly sweet, and gently sour.
description.de: Ein wild fermentiertes Māori-Brot, aufgebaut auf einem 'Kartoffel-Bug': Kartoffelpüree, Mehl und Zucker blubbern tagelang, bis sie voller natürlicher Hefen sind. In einen einfachen Teig eingearbeitet und in einer Kastenform gebacken, wird es weich und sanft säuerlich.
description.es: Un pan maorí de fermentación salvaje construido sobre un 'bicho de patata': puré de patata, harina y azúcar se dejan burbujear días hasta llenarse de levaduras naturales. Incorporado a una masa sencilla y horneado en molde, resulta suave, algo dulce y suavemente ácido.
description.fr: Un pain maori à fermentation sauvage bâti sur un 'bug de pomme de terre' : purée de pomme de terre, farine et sucre qu'on laisse buller pendant des jours jusqu'à grouiller de levures naturelles. Incorporé à une pâte simple et cuit en moule, il devient tendre et doucement acidulé.

origin.note: Adopted after European contact brought potatoes and wheat; the wild-fermented 'potato bug' starter became a distinctly Māori technique, still central to hui (gatherings) and marae meals today.

ingredients.note: active rewena potato bug (fermented potato, flour, sugar and water)

keeping.note: Keeps well thanks to its natural fermentation; a treasured Māori bread often served at gatherings and hui.

summary.en: Days ahead, mash boiled potato with flour, sugar and water and leave it somewhere warm to ferment until bubbling and nearly doubled, this is the starter.
Combine flour, sugar and salt, then work in the active potato bug and warm water.
Knead by hand or machine about 8 minutes until silky and supple; the dough stays soft and a little sticky.
Place in a buttered tin and let it rise until doubled, anywhere from ninety minutes to several hours depending on warmth.
Bake until deeply golden, finishing with a few minutes at a higher heat if more color is wanted.
Rest at least 30 minutes before slicing so the soft, gently sour crumb sets.

source.adaptation: The potato-bug starter is treated as one opaque ingredient; its internal flour and water aren't decomposed, so hydration reflects only the dough's own added water and flour.
